mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate declines by more than 50 basis points over a 3-month window, equity discount rates compress and forward equity valuations expand, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.91% as of Nov 2022 reflects recent tightening cycle
regime_hint: monetary easing or pause in tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the unemployment rate below 4.0% signals tight labor markets and wage-driven inflation expectations, which the equity market prices as a headwind to corporate margins and forward earnings, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of periods when UNRATE < 4.0%, or correlation between UNRATE and 60-day forward returns is positive and significant (p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month contraction in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand and forward economic growth, which equity markets interpret as earnings headwinds, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
regime_hint: demand slowdown or early recession signal
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above 1.5% year-over-year combined with inflation (CPIAUCSL) rising above 4.0% year-over-year signals stagflationary pressure. The market reprices equity risk premiums upward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO YoY growth > 1.5% AND CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 105.03 and CPIAUCSL at 243.85 as of Nov 2022
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances when both conditions are met, or the joint effect is not statistically significant (interaction p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 15% quarter-over-quarter, signaling a collapse in residential investment and forward demand, which cascades into negative multiplier effects on employment and consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 1,172K units as of Oct 2022
regime_hint: housing market downturn or credit tightening cycle
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of triggered instances, or the lead-lag relationship shows HOUST declines do not precede equity weakness by 60 days.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 100K new jobs per month while the unemployment rate remains stable, signaling labor market slack emerging and forward wage pressure easing. Equity markets interpret this as inflation-fighting progress, producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly growth < 100K AND UNRATE stable (±0.1pp)
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 146,135K and UNRATE at 4.3% as of Nov 2022
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in fewer than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 4.5% and inflation (CPIAUCSL) remains elevated above 3.5% year-over-year, the Fed is in restrictive policy stance. Equity markets price in extended earnings pressure from higher discount rates and slowing growth, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.91% and CPIAUCSL at 243.85 as of Nov 2022 reflect prior tightening
regime_hint: restrictive monetary policy regime with persistent inflation
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in fewer than 50% of instances when both conditions are met, or the joint restrictive signal does not outperform single-series signals in out-of-sample Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.0% annualized in the most recent quarter, signaling recession risk. Forward-looking equity investors reprice growth expectations downward, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the market anticipates earnings downgrades.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in fewer than 52% of triggered instances, or the lead-lag relationship shows GDPC1 slowdown does not precede equity weakness within 20 trading days.
